forms.notes: Dropped from Lesson 1 table — too obvious, student already knows it

sentence_patterns.note: The heart of tawhid — most common إِلَٰه pattern in the Qur'an
Prophets' repeated call — negating other gods

sentence_patterns.gloss: took his desire as his god
Is there a god with Allah?

verses.arabic_full: هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ۖ هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
verses.arabic_fragment: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ
verses.translation: He is Allah — there is no god but He
verses.score_notes: Short, iconic, contains إِلَٰه at the heart. Student memorises this one phrase.

verses.arabic_full: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِـۧمُ لِأَبِيهِ ءَازَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا ءَالِهَةً ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ
verses.arabic_fragment: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ آزَرَ أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْنَامًا آلِهَةً
verses.translation: And when Ibrahim said to his father Azar: Do you take idols as gods?
verses.score_notes: Story context: Ibrahim confronting his father about idols — iconic, every Muslim knows this story

verses.arabic_full: قُلِ ٱللَّهُمَّ مَـٰلِكَ ٱلْمُلْكِ تُؤْتِى ٱلْمُلْكَ مَن تَشَآءُ وَتَنزِعُ ٱلْمُلْكَ مِمَّن تَشَآءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَن تَشَآءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ بِيَدِكَ ٱلْخَيْرُ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
verses.translation: Say: O Allah, Owner of sovereignty
verses.score_notes: Introduces the prayer-address form. Trimmed to keep short.

verses.arabic_full: وَإِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
verses.translation: Your God is one God — there is no god but He, the Most Merciful, the Continuously Merciful
verses.score_notes: Triple إِلَٰه in one verse — the complete tawhid statement

verses.arabic_full: وَذَا ٱلنُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَـٰضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِى ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ أَن لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنتَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ إِنِّى كُنتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
verses.translation: He called out in the darkness: There is no god but You, glory be to You, I was among the wrongdoers
verses.score_notes: Story of Yunus in the whale — one of the most beloved du'as. Too long for beginner's first phrase but excellent for later lessons. Audio already downloaded.
verses.rejection_reason: Too long for a beginner's first phrase. Student found it overwhelming.

verses.arabic_full: لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ فَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ
verses.arabic_fragment: يَا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرُهُ
verses.translation: O my people, worship Allah — you have no god other than Him
verses.score_notes: Nuh's call — the same words repeated by every prophet. Pattern dropped from v2 table. Audio already downloaded.
verses.rejection_reason: Pattern (مِنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرُهُ) dropped from v2 table.

verses.arabic_full: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ · مَلِكِ النَّاسِ · إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ
verses.translation: Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind, the King of mankind, the God of mankind
verses.score_notes: Surah An-Nas — recited every day in salah and before sleep. Could revisit in a surah-based lesson. Audio already downloaded.

verses.arabic_full: فَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنۢبِكَ
verses.translation: Know that there is no god but Allah, and ask forgiveness for your sins
verses.score_notes: Direct command to the Prophet — most concise tawhid + istighfar in one breath. Audio already downloaded.

verses.arabic_full: قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ أَنَّمَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ
verses.translation: Say: I am only a human like you — it is revealed to me that your God is one God
verses.score_notes: Closing of Surah Al-Kahf — recited every Friday. Too long for Learn but good for Practice. Audio already downloaded.
verses.rejection_reason: Too long. Pattern dropped from v2 table.

verses.arabic_full: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَى ٱجْعَل لَّنَآ إِلَـٰهًا كَمَا لَهُمْ ءَالِهَةٌ
verses.translation: They said: O Musa, make for us a god like the gods they have
verses.score_notes: Israelites' shocking request — right after being saved from Pharaoh. Both singular and plural in one phrase. Audio already downloaded.
verses.rejection_reason: Replaced by shorter 6:74 fragment for آلِهَة.

verses.arabic_full: وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَأُ مَا عَلِمْتُ لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرِى
verses.translation: Pharaoh said: O chiefs, I know of no god for you other than myself
verses.score_notes: Anti-version of Nuh's call. Pharaoh flips the prophet's formula — ultimate arrogance. Audio already downloaded.
verses.rejection_reason: Very long. Pattern dropped from v2 table.

verses.arabic_full: شَهِدَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمِ
verses.translation: Allah bears witness that there is no god but He, and the angels, and those with knowledge
verses.target_lesson_reason: Ashhadu root (شَهِدَ) connects here — Allah Himself bearing witness
verses.score_notes: Allah Himself bearing witness — perfect for shahādah lesson. Cross-root with شَهِدَ.

verses.arabic_full: لَوْ كَانَ فِيهِمَآ ءَالِهَةٌ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ لَفَسَدَتَا
verses.translation: Had there been gods in them (heavens and earth) other than Allah, they would have been ruined
verses.score_notes: Famous logical argument for tawhid — short and self-contained.

verses.arabic_full: إِنَّنِىٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعْبُدْنِى وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِذِكْرِىٓ
verses.translation: Indeed, I am Allah — there is no god but Me, so worship Me and establish prayer for My remembrance
verses.score_notes: Allah speaking directly to Musa at the burning bush — powerful story, connects to salah

verses.arabic_full: إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوٓا۟ إِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
verses.translation: When it was said to them: There is no god but Allah — they were arrogant
verses.score_notes: Bridges both roots — la ilaha illa Allah + arrogance. Cross-root verse.

verses.arabic_full: أَجَعَلَ ٱلْـَٔالِهَةَ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَىْءٌ عُجَابٌ
verses.translation: Has he made the gods into one God? This is truly an astonishing thing!
verses.score_notes: Quraysh mocking 'One God?!' — dramatic, both singular and plural in one verse

verses.arabic_full: قُلِ ٱللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ عَـٰلِمَ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ أَنتَ تَحْكُمُ بَيْنَ عِبَادِكَ
verses.translation: Say: O Allah, Creator of the heavens and earth, Knower of the unseen and witnessed, You judge between Your servants
verses.score_notes: Beautiful du'a — Creator + Knower + Judge. Long but powerful.

hadith.title: Words Beloved to Allah
hadith.arabic: أَحَبُّ الْكَلَامِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَرْبَعٌ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، وَاللهُ أَكْبَرُ
hadith.translation: The most beloved words to Allah are four: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, La ilaha illallah, Allahu Akbar
hadith.score_notes: Bridges both roots — la ilaha illa Allah and Allahu Akbar together. Dropped when hadith section removed in v2.
